message:
perf(indexer): short-circuit rebuild_tantivy_from_db when checkpoint is already complete + idle-shutdown for aborted producer

The lexical-rebuild pipeline introduced in 6f411a47 performs meaningful
setup before it realizes the on-disk checkpoint already says the
rebuild is complete: it opens the Tantivy index, spawns the packet
producer, promotes the budget, warms a few prep workers, and only THEN
re-checks the checkpoint state. On large archives (2M+ messages) this
warm-up alone was measurably expensive — ~2-4s of wall-clock and a
burst of I/O — every single time `cass index` was run on an already-
indexed corpus with no new messages.

Two issues fixed here:

================================================================
1) Completed-checkpoint fast-path in rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options
================================================================

`src/indexer/mod.rs :: rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options`

The function already loaded the checkpoint state via
`load_checkpoint_state` as its first meaningful step. Previously the
`rebuild_state.completed` flag was only consulted indirectly — it
folded into `restart_from_zero` to decide whether to wipe the index
and start over, but the rest of the function ran either way. For a
repeat run where `completed == true` and all messages are already
committed, everything that followed — `TantivyIndex::open_or_create`,
the reader flush, the producer/consumer spawn, the flow reservation
setup, the page-prep worker pool start — was pure overhead.

Replaced with an up-front short-circuit immediately after checkpoint
load:

    if rebuild_state.completed
        || rebuild_state.committed_offset
           >= i64::try_from(total_conversations).unwrap_or(i64::MAX)
    {
        storage.close_without_checkpoint()?;
        progress.phase -> 0; is_rebuilding -> false;
        return Ok(LexicalRebuildOutcome {
            indexed_docs: rebuild_state.indexed_docs,
            observed_messages: Some(
                rebuild_state.db.total_messages.max(rebuild_state.indexed_docs)
            ),
            exact_checkpoint_persisted: false,
        });
    }

The `committed_offset >= total_conversations` half of the disjunction
catches the edge case where the persisted `completed` flag hasn't been
set yet but the offset already spans the full corpus — which can happen
when a prior rebuild crashed between the final page commit and the
flag write, and the rebuilder has since been restarted. Without this
half, that exact state would needlessly re-open Tantivy and start the
producer just to discover it has nothing to do.

`close_without_checkpoint` is the correct shutdown here — we held the
storage handle as read-only specifically to compute the fingerprint,
and since we wrote nothing there's nothing to checkpoint.

Also: the trailing `restart_from_zero` expression was simplified from

    let restart_from_zero = rebuild_state.completed
        || (rebuild_state.committed_offset == 0 && rebuild_state.pending.is_none());

to

    let restart_from_zero =
        rebuild_state.committed_offset == 0 && rebuild_state.pending.is_none();

since the `rebuild_state.completed` branch is now handled by the
fast-path above it.

================================================================
2) Idle-shutdown for aborted packet producer: shutdown_lexical_rebuild_packet_producer_after_startup_error
================================================================

`src/indexer/mod.rs :: shutdown_lexical_rebuild_packet_producer_after_startup_error`

New helper. Before this, if the producer thread had already been
spawned but a subsequent startup step failed (e.g. Tantivy open
returned a partial-index error that needed a from-zero restart, or a
reader flush panicked), the producer would be left dangling with a
live `Sender`. The pipeline drop wouldn't release the flow limiter
cleanly, and the producer thread could end up blocked forever on a
channel send that nobody was reading.

The new helper codifies the correct teardown sequence:

    flow_limiter.close();       // wake any pending reservations
    drop(pipeline_rx);          // break the channel — sender sees disconnect
    producer.join()             // collect the thread; log any panic payload

`panic_payload_message` is already in the crate and surfaces useful
`Box<dyn Any>` payloads as strings for the tracing warning, so
panics-during-abort get classified clearly in logs instead of being
silently swallowed.

================================================================
3) Tests
================================================================

`src/indexer/mod.rs` tests module

New `rebuild_tantivy_from_db_skips_tantivy_open_when_checkpoint_is_complete`
test (gated `#[serial]` like the rest of the rebuild suite). It:

  - Seeds a 2-conversation / 4-message fixture via
    `seed_lexical_rebuild_fixture` and the existing FTS schema helper
  - Writes a synthetic `lexical_rebuild_state.json` with
    `completed: true`, `committed_offset: 2`, full runtime snapshot
    zeroed out
  - Enables `CASS_PREP_PROFILE=1` to get the per-step
    `step="..."` log markers
  - Calls `rebuild_tantivy_from_db(..., 2, None)`, asserts the
    resulting outcome reports `indexed_docs = 4`, `observed_messages
    = Some(4)`, `exact_checkpoint_persisted = false`
  - Asserts the prep-step log stream does NOT contain
    `step="open_tantivy"`, `step="start_packet_producer"`, or
    `step="persist_initial_checkpoint"` — the entire hot path was
    skipped.

The existing prep-step assertions in the rest of the file were also
extended to whitelist the three newly-named markers
(`start_packet_producer`, `open_tantivy`, `persist_initial_checkpoint`)
so the fast-path test can negatively assert on them without the
positive-path tests accidentally failing when they legitimately emit
them.

No other files touched — this is a self-contained perf fix inside the
indexer crate, and the fast-path short-circuit doesn't alter the shape
of the `LexicalRebuildOutcome` or the `rebuild_tantivy_from_db` public
signature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
